root_text.an3.17:1.1: “Tayome, bhikkhave, dhammā attabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, parabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti.
root_text.an3.17:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhammā attabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, parabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti.
root_text.an3.17:2.1: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhammā nevattabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, na parabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti.
root_text.an3.17:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhammā nevattabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, na parabyābādhāyapi saṁvattanti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi saṁvattantī”ti.

translation_text.an3.17:1.1: “These three things, mendicants, lead to hurting yourself, hurting others, and hurting both.
translation_text.an3.17:1.3: Bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.17:1.4: These are three things that lead to hurting yourself, hurting others, and hurting both.
translation_text.an3.17:2.1: These three things, mendicants, don’t lead to hurting yourself, hurting others, or hurting both.
translation_text.an3.17:2.3: Good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.17:2.4: These are three things that don’t lead to hurting yourself, hurting others, or hurting both.”
